seoTitle: How to Get Typeform Notifications for New Responses
metaDescription: Set up email notifications and team alerts in Typeform, plus connect Slack or Zapier so you never miss a new form submission.
intro: Missing a new form submission can mean a slow response to a lead or customer request. This guide covers how to set up Typeform notifications so you and your team get alerted the moment a new response comes in, including connecting tools like Slack and Zapier.

steps.heading: Open notification settings
steps.body: Go to the notification settings for your Typeform to see the available options for alerts on new responses.

steps.heading: Enable email notifications
steps.body: Turn on email alerts so you receive a message every time someone submits your form.

steps.heading: Configure team alerts
steps.body: Add teammates or set up shared notifications so more than one person can respond quickly to new submissions.

steps.heading: Test notification setup
steps.body: Submit a test response to confirm that notifications arrive as expected before relying on the setup for real submissions.

faq.question: Can multiple team members receive Typeform notifications?
faq.answer: Yes, you can configure notifications so more than one team member is alerted when a new response comes in.

faq.question: Does Typeform integrate with Slack for notifications?
faq.answer: Yes, Typeform can connect with Slack and automation tools like Zapier to send alerts to channels or other apps beyond email.

faq.question: Will I get notified for every single response?
faq.answer: By default, notification settings can be configured to alert you for every new response, though you can adjust this based on your needs.

faq.question: How quickly do email notifications arrive after a submission?
faq.answer: Notifications are typically sent shortly after a response is submitted, helping you respond to leads or feedback promptly.
